marketState: neutral, leaning cautiously bullish — extreme 8-16 day heat forecast and stretched short COT positioning offset a bearish storage surplus and weak seasonality
summary: Price sits almost exactly at fair value today ($2.911 vs $2.923 fair, FAIR status) so there is no near-term value edge, but the fair-value curve itself is climbing to $3.050 by +21 days. The standout signal is weather: 8-16 day cooling demand is +30.5% above the 5-year norm and above the historical max range, pointing to a much hotter-than-normal stretch ahead, while 1-7 day demand is a milder +9.4% above normal. Managed money is net short at only the 8th percentile of its 5-year range, an extreme positioning skew that is vulnerable to short-covering if the heat verifies. Working against the bulls: storage is +180 Bcf above the 5-year average with a bearish build surprise (+22 Bcf vs forecast), and seasonality is low-reliability and mildly bearish (only 38% odds of higher prices in 4 weeks, typical 4-week move -1.4%). Technicals are split by timeframe — neutral/bullish intraday (5m-1h) but bearish on 4h/1d/1w — reinforcing a wait-for-confirmation posture short-term.

longTerm.entry: $2.80 – $2.95 (scale in on dips toward the fair-value midpoint)
longTerm.sizing: 1R approx 1% account risk; scale in over two tranches
longTerm.rationale: 8-16D cooling demand is +30.5% vs 5y and above the historical max range, while managed money is net short at just the 8th 5y percentile — a classic setup for short-covering into peak summer heat. Fair value keeps rising ($2.923 now to $3.050 by +21D) even though today's storage surplus (+180 Bcf, bearish build surprise) and weak seasonality (38% odds of higher prices in 4 weeks) argue for near-term caution.
longTerm.invalidation: The 8-16D heat forecast fades back toward normal, storage builds keep outpacing forecast (repeated bearish surprises), or price closes below roughly $2.65 confirming the seasonal downside case.

shortTerm.rationale: Front price is essentially at fair value ($2.911 vs $2.923 fair, FAIR status with only a -$0.012 spread) so there is no mispricing to trade, and technicals are split — neutral/bullish on 5m-1h but bearish on 4h/1d/1w. The 1-7D weather edge (+9.4% vs 5y demand) is real but not extreme enough alone to force an entry, and the front-to-next futures spread is only mildly backwardated (-$0.035), offering little confirmation of near-term tightness.
shortTerm.trigger: Wait for either a daily-trend flip (MACD histogram turns positive and RSI reclaims 50 on the 1d/4h) to confirm bullish momentum aligned with the heat forecast, or a dip toward $2.75-$2.80 (below fair value) for a discounted long entry.

Managed money net short at the 8th percentile of its 5-year range — extreme positioning, short-covering risk
